title: Pterocephalus afghanicus
intro: Pterocephalus afghanicus, also known as Afghan Pterocephalus, is a perennial plant species within the family Caprifoliaceae, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and aromatic foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and fever, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pterocephalus afghanicus include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or a decoction, which are commonly used to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and respiratory issues.

This family is part of the order Dipsacales, a group of flowering plants known for their four- or five-parted flowers. Pterocephalus afghanicus is a species of flowering plant within the genus Pterocephalus, which is a genus of plants found in Asia.
